channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The AI race is already taking a toll. Ireland’s massive data centers are a cautionary tale
item.description: Dozens of massive data centers humming at the outskirts of Dublin are consuming more electricity than all of the urban homes in Ireland and starting to wear out the warm welcome that brought them here.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Ireland’s AI data centers are sucking up too much of the country’s energy
item.description: Ireland is still heavily reliant on burning fossil fuels to generate electricity, despite a growing number of wind farms sprouting across the countryside. Further data center expansion threatens Ireland's goals to sharply cut planet-warming emissions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: How OpenAI’s Jerry Tworek found a new way forward for large language models
item.description: The impressive intelligence gains in OpenAI’s models over time have mainly come from training them with progressively more training data, for longer amounts of time, and with massive computing power.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: How drone activity could affect US holiday travel plans
item.description: Recent drone sightings around the country have raised concerns over whether drone activity could affect holiday travel
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, The Hill

item.title: Australian who falsely claimed to have invented bitcoin is found in contempt of UK court
item.description: LONDON (AP) — An Australian computer scientist who falsely claimed to be the founder of the bitcoin cryptocurrency was found Thursday to be in contempt of an order of London's High Court and was sentenced to 12 months in prison, suspended for two years.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Amazon workers to strike at multiple sites: Teamsters
item.description: The International Brotherhood of Teamsters union announced Amazon workers would strike starting Thursday in five confirmed states ahead of the Christmas holiday, citing the company’s absence from the bargaining negotiations as a reason.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Thousands of GPS tracking customers have info leaked following data breach
item.description: A bug in Hapn's website is allegedly spilling sensitive information, while the company remains silent.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Ireland embraced data centers that the AI boom needs. Now they're consuming too much of its energy
item.description: Dozens of massive data centers are consuming more electricity than all of the urban homes in Ireland and starting to wear out the warm welcome that brought them here.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Musk pressure campaign on CR highlights his growing influence
item.description: Elon Musk’s aggressive push against a stopgap measure to prevent a government shutdown received mixed reactions from House Republicans, even as it seemingly ended in success with President-elect Trump and Vice President-elect Vance announcing their own opposition to the measure.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Sony announces new 'Amethyst' collaboration with AMD to develop 'machine learning' technology in games
item.description: PlayStation console architect Mark Cerny has announced a new collaboration between Sony and AMD codenamed 'Amethyst'.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: House Passes Continuing Resolution, Bipartisan Bills Ahead of Lame-Duck Session
item.description: Congress passes continuing resolution to keep government funded through March 14, while lawmakers consider reforms to federal hiring practices and IT contracts with China-based companies.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: How the Trump administration is likely to deal with artificial intelligence
item.description: The National Defense Authorization Act for 2025 has extensive language on ensuring competition in AI. What can we expect from the Trump administration? The post How the Trump administration is likely to deal with artificial intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk and Trump are viewed roughly the same by Americans, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk, clad in tuxedo and black tie, took the stage at President-elect Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort shortly after the election with all the swagger of the winning candidate himself. “The public has given us a mandate that could not be more clear, the clearest mandate.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, SARAH PARVINI and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CISA directs agencies to find, fix cloud security misconfigurations
item.description: CISA is telling agencies they need to follow the "SCuBA" cloud security configurations for widely used applications, including Microsoft 365 products. The post CISA directs agencies to find, fix cloud security misconfigurations first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: EU investigates TikTok over Romanian presidential election safeguards
item.description: LONDON (AP) — European Union regulators said Tuesday they're investigating whether TikTok breached the bloc's digital rulebook by failing to deal with risks to Romania's presidential election, which has been thrown into turmoil over allegations of electoral violations and Russian meddling.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate report alleges Amazon rejected warehouse safety recommendations due to productivity concerns
item.description: At least two internal Amazon studies found a link between how quickly the online retailer's warehouse workers perform tasks and workplace injuries, but the company rejected many safety recommendations out of concern the proposed changes might reduce productivity, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CompTIA introduces foundational tech certification
item.description: CompTIA has created a certification designed to provide individuals with broad foundational and digital skills that can be used to build a career in technology.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google, Microsoft could turn gatekeepers for advanced AI chips
item.description: The US government could designate cloud providers including Google and Microsoft as global gatekeepers for advanced AI chips, a step widely viewed as the latest effort to block China’s access to this critical technology, according to a Reuters report.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What IT hiring looks like heading into 2025
item.description: Despite low unemployment in the IT industry, IT hiring has slowed over the past two years and is expected to plateau in 2025 as employers prioritize experienced candidates with specialized skills, according to three new studies.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 10 quantum computing milestones of 2024
item.description: There was a time, back in the early days of computing, when we had vacuum tubes, relays, mechanical gears plus several different types of analog computers. We’re kind of in that same era today with quantum computers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Eyewitness accounts of New Jersey drones include politicians, journalist
item.description: Here are some of the firsthand accounts of the drone sightings, as told to NewsNation by eyewitnesses.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Court denies TikTok's request to halt enforcement of potential US ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday left in place a mid-January deadline in a federal law requiring TikTok to be sold or face a ban in the United States, rejecting a request made by the company to halt enforcement until the Supreme Court reviews its challenge of the statute.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — From answering questions from Cabinet ministers, academics and students on climate change, substance abuse and the law to children’s inquiries about her “birth” and links to God and being described as a talkative feminist, Sophia, the world-famous robot won hearts at an innov
item.dc:creator: BY FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Metaphor: ReFantazio, Dragon Age, Astro Bot and an indie wave lead the top AP video games of 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s been a rough year in the world of video games, which reeled from some high-profile flops (sorry, Suicide Squad and Star Wars fans) and a relentless series of developer layoffs. Fortunately, indie designers stepped up to fill in the gaps.
item.dc:creator: LOU KESTEN, Associated Press, KRON4
